summary: Typosquat of tslib with remote-eval backdoor in exported API
details: Package impersonates Microsoft's `tslib` (TypeScript runtime helpers) — the README is copy-pasted from the official `tslib` project — but ships none of the advertised helpers. The exported `buildoptimize` function in index.js iterates two hardcoded URLs (`https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/` and `https://verceljs-kappa.vercel.app/icons/sx/`), fetches each via `request`, JSON-parses the response body and passes it directly to `eval()` (index.js:78 `eval(JSON.parse(b));`). A 10-minute `setTimeout` is then scheduled, consistent with a beacon/persistence loop. There is no content validation, no signature/hash pinning, and the fetched content is unrelated to the advertised TypeScript-helper purpose. Any consumer that calls `buildoptimize()` runs arbitrary attacker-controlled JavaScript in their Node process under the operator of the Vercel deployment, which is an author-mutable third-party host. The combination of name-confusion against `tslib`, fraudulent README, and remote-eval of mutable third-party content in the package's primary exported API is an active backdoor against installers.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
